screens.name: Songs index
screens.purpose: Let a user scan available copied charts and open a focused song detail route.

screens.name: Song detail
screens.purpose: Render one copied chart as a lead-sheet style detail page.
screens.primary_action: Return to songs or read the chart.

ux_rules: Remove temporary or sample-only rows from user-facing song discovery when they are not intended as real copied charts.
Keep the list row hierarchy and metadata format unchanged for remaining songs.
Preserve the existing empty and not-found states rather than introducing new UI behavior.
Use browser-backed verification to confirm the sample is absent and representative remaining rows still fit the available width.

screen_contexts.layout_intent: Maintain a dense but calm list of real copied chart rows with stable row spacing, metadata columns, and no extra local labels.
interaction_risks: Temporary sample entries create misleading choice and inflate chart counts.
Removing a row must not leave stale counts or broken links.
notes: Before evidence: /songs listed 잔향 as a renderer sample and linked it to /songs/janhyang-photo-sample.

screen_contexts.layout_intent: Keep existing lead-sheet detail rendering available for remaining songs and return not-found behavior for removed song IDs.
interaction_risks: Direct sample route could remain reachable if the data cleanup is incomplete.
Renderer cleanup must not remove shared detail components used by remaining songs.

constraints: Remove only the temporary janhyang-photo-sample data entry from src/lib/songs.ts.
Do not change list component layout, colors, spacing, or typography.
Do not remove the lead-sheet renderer or any remaining song entries.
Keep aggregate counts data-driven from the remaining songs array.
Preserve existing getSongById behavior so removed IDs fall through to the existing not-found detail state.
Verify at least one remaining detail route still renders.

critical_judgments: The temporary sample is absent from the rendered list.
The remaining rows are still scannable and fit without avoidable horizontal overflow.
The header count reflects the remaining public songs.
must_not_regress: Existing copied chart rows stay linked.
No duplicate local labels are introduced.
The empty state remains valid if songs later becomes empty.

critical_judgments: A real remaining detail route renders without console or overlay errors.
The removed sample route is no longer generated by songs data and falls back to not-found behavior.
must_not_regress: ScoreViewer and LeadSheetRenderer continue to compile.
Back-to-songs navigation remains visible on detail pages.

visual_constraints: No new semantic CSS classes are introduced.
No visual restyling is planned; after-browser verification should show row removal only.
Verification must check representative list rows, redundant labels, and available-width fit.
